title: GTM Persona
description: A GTM-native synthetic persona — the shared substrate behind 'build the persona once, consume everywhere'. One JSON file per persona, stored at `.gtm/personas/<id>.json`. This is the GTM-minimal projection of the richer SRD persona (re-themed down from `.context/srd-source/schemas/persona.schema.yml` to the 9 SDV-relevant fields, plus optional skepticism). Product-internal SRD fields — lifecycle, primary_journeys, churn_risk_moments, scores, conversion_trigger — are intentionally TRIMMED; this substrate exists to feed demand and targeting, not to model the product. The SDV panel (the demand-forecaster agent / `skills/synthetic-demand/`) is the primary consumer: the presence of personas here promotes an SDV run from F0 cold-start to F1 persona-grounded (see `skills/synthetic-demand/rules/fidelity-tiers.md`). Media buying (lookalike seeds, PostHog cohorts, interest stacks) and email marketing (segment objections + price tolerance) consume the same objects. NEVER write SRD's schema from GTM; if an external `srd/personas.yml` exists, read it and project DOWN into this shape (read-only).

id.description: Stable persona ID, format 'P01', 'P02', ... Sequential, unique within `.gtm/personas/`. Matches the filename (`.gtm/personas/<id>.json`) and the IDs referenced by SDV concepts (`target_segments`), media-buyer seeds, and email segments.

age.description: A specific age, not a range — picks the central buyer for this segment.

income.description: Monthly income with currency, e.g. '$800/month'. Free-text so regional currencies (LATAM, etc.) survive the projection.

location.description: 'City, Country' format, e.g. 'Bogota, Colombia'. Used for ad geo-targeting and to flag out-of-distribution / regional-pricing surfaces in SDV (a low familiarity_score widens the bands).

goals.description: What this persona wants to achieve with the product. Feeds SDV appeal/comprehension priming and email value framing.

pain_points.description: Frustrations with current alternatives — the demand the product addresses. Seeds SDV objection mining and email/ad pain-led hooks.

monthly_spend.description: Typical monthly spend in this category (dollar amount or range), e.g. '$50-$120'. A budget-salience anchor for the SDV skeptic stance and for offer/price tolerance reads.

user_pct.description: Share of the total user base this persona represents, as a percentage (0-100). Across ALL personas in `.gtm/personas/`, user_pct SHOULD sum to approximately 100% (see consistency notes). Used to volume-weight the SDV panel and size addressable audiences.

revenue_pct.description: Share of total revenue this persona represents, as a percentage (0-100). Across ALL personas in `.gtm/personas/`, revenue_pct SHOULD sum to approximately 100% (see consistency notes). Used to revenue-weight comparative rankings and to size objection revenue-at-risk (`segment.revenue_pct x target MRR`).

price_sensitivity.description: How much price moves this persona's purchase decision. Primes the SDV price test (Van Westendorp / Gabor-Granger) and email price-tolerance enrichment. Defaults to 'medium' when unknown.

skepticism.description: Optional. How resistant this persona is to marketing claims. Primes the SDV skeptic stance (an anti-positivity defense). When omitted, the SDV panel applies the default skeptic stance ('medium') — this substrate never makes a respondent more credulous than the skeptic baseline.
